name: Dried milk / whole milk powder (food medium)
basis: per 100 g dry whole milk powder

components.name: lactose
components.source: USDA FDC 173454 (total sugars 38.42 g, ~100% lactose in bovine milk)
components.evidence: Total sugars in whole milk powder are essentially all lactose; DOMINANT fermentable carbon source of the medium.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Only trace free glucose/galactose in unfermented milk powder; present but unquantified.

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence: Trace free galactose in unfermented powder; unquantified.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Supplee & Bellis / Garrett bulk-milk mean 0.172% (1.72 g/L) scaled x7.9 to powder
components.evidence: Citrate is the principal organic acid of milk (~0.172 g/100 mL fluid); concentrated ~8x in powder. KEY OMISSION added; secondary carbon/energy source and Ca-chelator.

components.name: orotic acid
components.source: Larsen/Agrarforschung Schweiz 2008; PMC8614319: cow milk 6.3-7.6 mg/100g fluid, scaled x7.9
components.evidence: Pyrimidine-precursor organic acid characteristic of bovine milk (~6.5 mg/100 g fluid); concentrated ~8x. KEY OMISSION added.

components.name: lactic acid
components.evidence: Fresh/dried (unfermented) milk contains only trace lactic acid (<0.02 g/100 mL fluid); present but minimal, included for completeness.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.source: USDA FDC 173454 protein 26.32 g; AA profile Prosser et al. PMC11640299 (208.5 mg/g protein)
components.evidence: Total (protein-bound + free) glutamate; most abundant amino acid in casein/whey.

components.name: L-proline
components.source: PMC11640299 96.0 mg/g protein x 26.32 g protein
components.evidence: Total amino acid, acid hydrolysis of protein.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Total amino acid (L-serine; replaces mis-assigned D-serine).

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: ~80% casein / 20% whey; substrate for amino-acid supply above.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Milk fat, breakdown by fatty acid below.

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: Dominant saturated fatty acid of milk fat.

components.name: butyric acid (4:0)
components.evidence: Short-chain FA characteristic of milk fat.

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence: Genuine milk sterol (animal food) - correctly retained, NOT noise here.

components.name: calcium
components.evidence: Largely as colloidal calcium phosphate in casein micelles.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Consolidated single iron entry (replaces duplicated Fe2+/Fe3+/'Fe2+ mitochondria').

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Major anion of milk serum; present but not in USDA panel, unquantified here.

components.name: iodine
components.evidence: Trace element in milk; present, unquantified.

components.name: riboflavin (B2)
components.evidence: Milk is a rich riboflavin source.

components.name: niacin (B3)
components.evidence: Preformed niacin (milk also supplies tryptophan precursor).

components.name: vitamin D
components.source: USDA FDC 173454 (0.5 ug/100g, without added vit D)

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Present in milk; not in USDA panel, unquantified here.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Genuine milk-fat carotenoid pigment; present, unquantified.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: Milk protein contains L-serine, not the D-enantiomer; D-serine is a FooDB stereochemical mis-assignment. Replaced with L-serine.

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Stereochemical artifact/duplicate of L-isoleucine; not a distinct milk nutrient.

remove.name: Nonanoate
remove.reason: C9:0 (nonanoic acid) is not a meaningful bovine milk-fat fatty acid; FooDB trace/predicted noise (real odd-chain milk FAs are C15:0/C17:0).

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Subcellular-compartment artifact duplicating iron; consolidated into a single 'iron' entry.

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: Microbial food-preservative additive, not a native constituent of milk; FooDB noise.

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: Plant furanocoumarin (Apiaceae/citrus); biologically implausible in bovine milk; FooDB predicted, not measured.

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: Trace Strecker/flavor volatile aldehyde, not a growth-relevant nutrient.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Ultra-trace non-nutritive contaminant element, not a defined milk growth nutrient.

missing_added: citric acid (~1.36 g/100g) - principal milk organic acid, was absent
orotic acid (~0.051 g/100g) - characteristic milk pyrimidine acid, was absent
lactose quantified (38.4 g/100g) - dominant fermentable sugar
full quantitative amino-acid profile (18 AAs with g/100g)
individual fatty acids with amounts (16:0, 18:1, 14:0, 18:0, 4:0, etc.)
vitamins and minerals with real USDA amounts
lactic acid flagged as trace (unfermented product)
keep_uncovered: beta-Lactose (anomer of lactose; genuine, folds into lactose amount)
Myristoleic acid (14:1) (genuine trace milk fatty acid; no priority BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 173454 (Milk, dry, whole, without added vitamin D)
Prosser et al., 'Amino Acid Composition of Dried Bovine Dairy Powders', PMC11640299
Supplee & Bellis, 'Citric acid content of milk and milk products', J Biol Chem (bulk-milk mean 0.172%)
Larsen / Agrarforschung Schweiz 2008 and PMC8614319 (orotic acid in cow milk 6.3-7.6 mg/100 g fluid)
notes: DOMINANT carbon/energy source is lactose (38.4 g/100g); citric acid (~1.36 g/100g) and orotic acid (~0.051 g/100g) are the key organic-acid omissions now added (scaled from fluid-milk concentrations by the ~8x powder concentration factor, protein 3.3%->26.3%). Milk protein (26.3 g) supplies the full amino-acid pool and milk fat (26.7 g, palmitate/oleate-rich) the lipids. Cholesterol is retained as a genuine animal-food sterol. Amino acids are total (acid-hydrolysis) values, not free.
